bullets.text: Q1 companywide CR 86.1, dramatically better than 96.0 target; Personal Lines CR 84.5 vs. 98.7 in Q1 2023; personal auto frequency down ~9% YOY for both bodily injury and physical damage; 0.7 pts favorable prior-year development.

Companywide NPW grew 18% YOY; Personal Lines NPW up 20%; Direct auto tied all-time record quote volume in Q1 with largest single-day quote volume ever recorded; phone sales set daily and weekly new application records.

Personal Lines PIFs reached 26.5 million, up 7% YOY; ten consecutive months of YOY improvement in policy life expectancy; non-rate restrictions continued to be unwound as profitability improved across states.

$500M preferred shares redeemed during Q1, reflecting strong capital generation; Q1 investment return 0.8% (fixed income 0.3%, equity 9.9%); duration extended to 3.2 years; positioned to significantly grow business.

Property rates increased ~6% in Q1 (17% trailing four quarters); personal auto rates considered adequate in most states; focus shifting from rate actions to growth with competitive pricing; cautious return to normal business operations.

Property Q1 CR 93.4, improving 12.1 points YOY; catastrophe losses down 5.0 pts from Q1 2023; Florida favorable development linked to tort reform legislation passed in Q1 2023 reducing loss frequency.

Auto model 8.9 elevated in first state with 20 more states planned for 2024; model 8.8 in 27 states (>60% of NPW); property 5.0 model in 5 states (13% of home premium); BOP launched in Florida (45th state, 76% of CL multiperil market).

Commercial Lines Q1 CR 91.8 (6.6 pts better YOY); commercial rate increases of ~17% in 2023 earning through; for-hire transportation BMT declining with freight market contraction; business auto and contractor BMTs showing healthy shopping growth.
Property PIFs reached 3.2 million, up 10% YOY; property NPW up 17%; growth focused in less catastrophe-prone states, up 20% YOY there vs. down 5% in high-volatility states.

Progressive earned Gallup Exceptional Workplace Award for the fourth consecutive year; employee stories highlighted culture of support through personal tragedy and community engagement through new volunteer time-off benefit.
